mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 45%) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflect supply-side shocks that immediately depress equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening regimes yields positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, invalidating the supply shock transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases household income and corporate revenue projections, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening final demand, which suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, refuting the inventory glut mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which rapidly compresses equity multiples and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of cases following CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Improvements in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signal restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth in recovery regimes fails to predict positive 20-day returns (win rate < 40%) in walk-forward validation.
